cards.back: A mathematical model for a computer that consists of a tape divided into cells, each of which can hold a symbol, and a read/write head that can move along the tape and change the symbol at each cell
A set of instructions that a computer follows to solve a problem or perform a task
A system is Turing complete if it can simulate the behavior of a Turing machine, meaning it can solve any problem that a Turing machine can solve
A type of grammar used to define the structure of programming languages, where the production rules are based on the context in which the symbols appear
A classification of context-free grammars based on their generative power, ranging from simple regular languages to more complex context-free languages
